item.title: Bayesian Additive Regression Trees
item.description: Definition: Variant of Bagging and Boosting (Machine Learning) for trees where boosting occurs via making small changes to pre-existing trees in order to better fit Residuals ...

item.title: Temporary Notes for Jonathan Wang
item.description: I’m testing Quartz to publish some notes I’ve collected in Obsidian.

item.title: Classification
item.description: Definition: A type of Supervised Machine Learning where the output space is a set of CLASSES (mutually exclusive) \mathcal{Y} = \{1, 2, ..., C\} Examples: Binary Classification ...

item.title: Machine Learning
item.description: Definition: A computer program that learns from some experience on some tasks according to performance metric where performance on tasks increases with experience ...

item.title: Manipulating Financial Data in Python
item.description: Notes: Why python? Good scientific libraries Fast if using matrices. What does a CSV file look like for stock data? We work with CSV files. HCP.csv DateOpenHighLowCloseVolumeAdj Closedateopen pricehigh pricelow priceactual end price# shares tradedclose, adjusted for stock splits and dividendsNEWER (2012-09-11)46.2346.7846.2046.73185540046.73…OLDER (2000-02-01)24.8725.1524.4425.004133005.36 (NOTE the large historical difference to close) Note that we only care about info that changes over time and are relevant for stock price info.
